game_name: Police Quest: In Pursuit of the Death Angel

danger_signals.visual_form: Black Cadillac (or Mercedes-Benz in remake), later repainted light blue
danger_signals.meaning: Active criminal element; vehicle linked to drug dealing and murder

danger_signals.visual_form: Crashed vehicle with deceased driver (Lonny West)
danger_signals.meaning: Escalation of danger; homicide indicates organized crime violence

danger_signals.visual_form: Group of motorcycle riders causing trouble at establishment
danger_signals.meaning: Street-level threat; public disorder and potential violence
Public safety hazard; immediate threat to civilians

danger_signals.visual_form: Jason Taselli identity reveal; presence at high-level crime locations
danger_signals.meaning: Major criminal threat; escalated jurisdiction and danger level
Direct evidence of narcotics trafficking; organized crime involvement
System failure; dangerous criminal at large
Internal gang violence; power struggle within criminal hierarchy

safety_signals.visual_form: Proper gun handling sequence (draw, aim, then fire); weapon storage in locker
safety_signals.meaning: Following protocol maintains player safety and mission success

safety_signals.visual_form: Officer Sonny Bonds' identification as law enforcement
safety_signals.meaning: Legitimate authority; player is on the side of order

safety_signals.visual_form: Narcotics Division team responding to undercover call at penthouse
safety_signals.meaning: Institutional support; danger mitigated through proper channels
Safe space; community and peer support among law enforcement
Earned authority; progression through proper ranks increases capability

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Police station; patrol routes through Lytton; Blue Room café (off-duty gathering); jail facility
Hotel Delphoria (undercover stakeout location); card game venue; penthouse suite (operation endpoint)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Stolen vehicles (black Cadillac); drug paraphernalia; firearms (.45 Smith & Wesson)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Jason Taselli (FBI Most Wanted); Frank Magpie (front man); Lonny West (low-level dealer, murdered); Donald Colby (street-level dealer)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hotel Delphoria (gambling operation front); penthouse suite (command center); river (body disposal location); bar (information/recruitment hub)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Most Wanted List designation; no-bail warrant authority
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Jurisdiction over high-level criminals like Taselli; inter-agency coordination with local police

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Rising crime in Lytton due to drug epidemic
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased traffic violations; bar incidents; presence of stolen vehicles; drug-addicted civilians (Kathy Cobb in overdose coma)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Sonny assigned to traffic duty initially; escalation of patrol incidents; visible desperation among citizens

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Speeding motorist; drunk driver; biker gang at bar; prostitution (Sweet Cheeks Marie)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Routine patrol responses; traffic enforcement; bar management intervention

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stolen vehicle recovery; murder investigation; FBI Most Wanted suspect identification; drugs in trunk
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Case escalation; promotion to detective; formation of investigative partnership (Bonds + Watts)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Taselli escapes custody; body discovered in river; internal power consolidation around Bains
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Undercover operation launched; infiltration of gambling ring; exposure of command structure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Penthouse showdown; identity revelation; backup team arrival; criminal leader shot/arrested
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Bains arrested and convicted (97-year sentence); Bonds awarded Key to the City; resolution of Kathy Cobb crisis through criminal justice

environmental_storytelling.example: Crashed vehicle with dead dealer (Lonny West) communicates the brutality of the drug trade and establishes murder as central plot driver
Black Cadillac repainted light blue signals criminal attempt to obscure evidence and evade police detection
Blue Room café as police safe space vs. Hotel Delphoria as criminal den establishes moral geography of Lytton
Kathy Cobb's overdose coma and death show civilian collateral damage, motivating Sonny's personal vendetta against Bains
Promotion from traffic officer to detective correlates with access to undercover operations and greater institutional resources
Taselli's corpse in the river indicates internal gang violence and elimination of competing faction members
Sweet Cheeks Marie transitions from prostitute/informant to trusted partner, showing relationship-building across class boundaries

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Traffic enforcement; responding to accidents; bar management
Vehicle recovery; evidence collection; suspect interrogation
Infiltrating gambling ring; gaining criminal trust; maintaining false identity
Passive (crime scene discovery only); signals high-level violence in criminal hierarchy
Fleeing in stolen vehicle; carrying weapons; engaged in drug trafficking
At large; eliminated by rival gang (corpse found in river)
Maintaining front operations; conducting card games; recruiting trusted associates; suspicious of undercover infiltration
Attempting to kill discovered undercover officer; neutralized by backup team
Recruiting players into gambling ring; vetting new members; reporting to Bains
Providing intelligence on drug ring; facilitating undercover access; developing romantic bond with Sonny
Overdose; comatose; death (off-screen); motivates father (Officer Cobb) and Sonny's vendetta
Distributing drugs to addicted users; arrested during stakeout; represents low-tier criminal operation
Assigning traffic duty; delegating initial murder investigation; institutional authority figure
Joint narcotics investigation; stakeout operations; institutional support for Sonny's cases

notes: Police Quest emphasizes procedural realism as core gameplay mechanic. Danger signals are tightly integrated with gameplay consequences—failure to follow proper police procedure (e.g., not storing firearms) results in immediate player death, making world-state manifestation inseparable from mechanical punishment. The game uses a rising crime spiral (low-level traffic → organized narcotics → crime lord showdown) to visually communicate escalating danger. Faction indicators rely heavily on environmental location (precinct vs. hotel) and character association rather than color/symbol systems. The 1987 AGI version used text-based commands; the 1992 SCI remake added point-and-click visuals, but thematic manifestation patterns remain consistent across versions.
sources_used: Wikipedia - Police Quest: In Pursuit of the Death Angel
